explanations.6: This pattern matches any email address where something comes before @newsletter and then any domain after the dot — for example, spam@newsletter.com would match.
explanations.7: This line is missing a comma at the end, which will cause a syntax error when the file is parsed.
explanations.8: This pattern matches emails from any domain where the sender is literally named newsletters-noreply, like newsletters-noreply@example.com.
